channel.title: Dong Xing's Blog
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Dong Xing's Blog

item.title: Inverse Fast Mobius Transform
item.description: The Inverse Fast Mobius Transform is an algorithm that finds a function g such that \forall S\subseteq\{0,1,\dots,n-1\},\sum_{T\subseteq S}g(T)=f(S) in \mathcal{O}(n2^n) time and \mathcal{O}(1) space.

item.title: Fast Mobius Transform
item.description: The Fast Mobius Transform is an algorithm that computes f(S)=\sum_{T\subseteq S}g(T) for a function g defined on \mathcal{P}(\{0,1,\dots,n-1\}) in \mathcal{O}(n2^n) time and \mathcal{O}(1) space.

item.title: Euclidean Algorithm
item.description: The Euclidean Algorithm is an algorithm that computes \gcd\{a, b\} for non-negative integers a and b in \mathcal{O}(\log a+\log b) time and \mathcal{O}(\log a+\log b) space.

item.title: Extended Euclidean Algorithm
item.description: The Extended Euclidean Algorithm is an algorithm that finds a pair of integers x and y such that ax+by=\gcd\{a,b\} for non-negative integers a and b in \mathcal{O}(\log a+\log b) time and \mathcal{O}(\log a+\log b) space.

item.title: Inverse Fast Fourier Transform
item.description: The Inverse Fast Fourier Transform is an algorithm that finds a polynomial f(x)=\sum_{i=0}^{n-1}a_ix^i such that \forall k\in\{0,1,\dots,n-1\},f(\omega_n^k)=b_k, if \exist k\in\N,n=2^k, in \mathcal{O}(n\log n) time and \mathcal{O}(1) space.

item.title: Fast Fourier Transform
item.description: The Fast Fourier Transform is an algorithm that computes f(\omega_n^0),f(\omega_n^1),\dots,f(\omega_n^{n-1}) for a polynomial f(x)=\sum_{i=0}^{n-1}a_ix^i, if \exist k\in\N,n=2^k, in \mathcal{O}(n\log n) time and \mathcal{O}(1) space.

item.title: Cartesian Tree
item.description: The Cartesian Tree is a data structure that maintains an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} by maintaining a binary tree.

item.title: Treap
item.description: The Treap is a data structure that maintains an array of n numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} by maintaining a randomized Cartesian Tree.

item.title: Trie
item.description: The Trie is a data structure that maintains a set S of strings in \Sigma^* by maintaining a deterministic finite automaton that accepts and only accepts strings in S.

item.title: Bellman-Ford Algorithm
item.description: The Bellman-Ford Algorithm is an algorithm that computes the length of the shortest path from a vertex s to every vertex in a directed graph G=(V,E) with edge weights and no negative cycles in \mathcal{O}(|V||E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|) space.
